creator: dteske@FreeBSD.org
text:
It has been discussed that this breaks service(8) and will need to be backed out. The patch is wrong for 3 reasons:

1. service(8) needs to be always aligned to generate an identical environment to run rc.d scripts compared to /etc/rc invocation at boot. This patch, without a corresponding patch in-kind to /etc/rc introduces a dichotomy where multi-user mode and the boot-process itself can (and do) produce different situations.

2. It introduces a variable (TERM) without a constant value. It's not against the law to introduce a new variable to the set of environment variables offered to rc.d scripts, but said variables need to have constant values to reinforce rule #1 [above].

3. The patch to service(8) doesn't update the manual wherein it is described that $HOME and $PATH are the only contrived variables set for the child environment (and in the spirit of #2 above, the values are static for providing a consistent environment across the duo of boot versus service(8) invocation; and in the spirit of #1 above, it is $HOME and $PATH to align with /etc/rc).

This (backing out the patch; SVN r268641) however still leaves programmers writing rc.d scripts with the open question of "how do I run screen/tmux from an rc.d script?" (specifically, without hitting the error "open terminal failed: terminal does not support clear" [tmux] or "Please set a terminal type." [screen]).

NB: I've been told this affects bhyve, puppet, and minecraft developers/maintainers.

The quick and dirty answer is the above is to add the "-d" flag to your screen/tmux invocations to cause the newly formed session to start "detached". For example, instead of trying the following:

/usr/local/bin/tmux new-session -s my_session_name ls

(which, if invoked with the prefix "env -i " will reproduce the mentioned error)

Instead do the following:

/usr/local/bin/tmux new-session -s my_session_name -d ls

If you're using screen, change from:

/usr/local/bin/screen -s my_session_name ls

To instead:

/usr/local/bin/screen -s my_session_name -dm ls

NOTE: Unlike tmux which only requires the addition of "-d", screen needs "-dm" ("-d -m" if you're feeling verbose; without the "-m" screen will think you want to attach to an already-existing but detached session).

However, adding "-d" [tmux] or "-dm" [screen] may not be what you want (the net-effect is that now your command launches detached). If you still desire to start a session attached, it is recommended that you (in your rc.d script) manually set the TERM environment variable to a known-good value. Below are the known-good values:

For FreeBSD 9.0 and higher:

        export TERM=xterm

For older versions (8.x and below):

        export TERM=cons25

With the addition of this one-line you should be able to start a session attached via rc.d script.
